skill.name: cohort-study-quality-assessment-nos
skill.description: Evaluates the quality of cohort studies using the Newcastle-Ottawa Scale (NOS). Use when the user provides a cohort study article or text and needs a quality assessment report.
skill.summary: Applies the Newcastle-Ottawa Scale to score cohort study quality across Selection, Comparability, and Outcome criteria.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: Static scanner flagged 68 potential issues but all HIGH findings are false positives (asterisk characters used as NOS star ratings misidentified as weak cryptographic algorithms). The 39 external_commands findings are markdown-formatted bash examples that the AI is instructed to execute; this is intentional and documented skill behavior. The actual risk is MEDIUM: the skill instructs AI agents to run Python scripts with user-provided data, which requires careful input sanitization. No credential theft, data exfiltration, or malicious intent detected. Skill is safe to publish with appropriate warnings about external command execution.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell command execution with user-provided data
dangerous_patterns.description: SKILL.md instructs AI to run python scripts/calculate_nos_score.py with JSON data passed as command-line argument. User-supplied study assessment data flows through shell command execution, which could be exploited for command injection if the JSON is not properly escaped.

dangerous_patterns.title: PDF file read and text extraction
dangerous_patterns.description: scripts/extract_pdf.py reads PDF files from user-provided file paths and writes extracted text to disk. The script accepts file paths from command-line arguments without path sanitization beyond resolving and quote stripping.

content.user_title: Assess Cohort Study Quality with NOS Criteria
content.value_statement: Medical researchers need a fast, consistent way to evaluate cohort study quality for systematic reviews. This skill applies the Newcastle-Ottawa Scale to produce a structured quality assessment report with star ratings and documented reasoning.
actual_capabilities: Evaluate cohort study quality against all 9 NOS criteria across Selection, Comparability, and Outcome categories
Extract text from PDF files and assess the study content against NOS standards
Calculate star scores and generate markdown summary tables with per-criterion results
Classify study quality as high (7-9 stars), moderate (4-6 stars), or low (fewer than 4 stars)
Document reasoning for each scoring decision to support transparency and auditability
limitations: Only evaluates cohort studies, not case-control studies, randomized trials, or cross-sectional designs
Requires full study text or a complete PDF to produce an accurate assessment
Cannot access paywalled articles, subscription databases, or restricted full-text content
Automated scoring does not replace domain expert judgment for nuanced quality evaluation

use_cases.title: Systematic Review Quality Screening
use_cases.description: A researcher conducting a systematic review needs to screen multiple cohort studies for inclusion. This skill provides consistent NOS scoring across all studies with documented reasoning.

use_cases.title: Grant Proposal Study Appraisal
use_cases.description: A grant applicant needs to justify the quality of cohort studies cited in a research proposal. The skill generates structured quality evidence for each referenced study.

use_cases.title: Peer Review Quality Verification
use_cases.description: A journal reviewer needs to verify quality claims about cohort studies in a submitted manuscript. The skill produces independent NOS-based assessments for each study.

prompt_templates.title: Basic NOS Assessment from Abstract
prompt_templates.prompt: Assess this PubMed abstract of a cohort study using NOS. Determine what information is available and what is missing for each of the 9 criteria.
prompt_templates.scenario: User pastes a study abstract and needs a quick quality check

prompt_templates.title: Full Assessment from PDF
prompt_templates.prompt: I have a cohort study PDF. Extract the text, then evaluate it using NOS criteria. Score each of the 9 items and provide reasoning for each.
prompt_templates.scenario: User provides a PDF file path for comprehensive evaluation

prompt_templates.title: Detailed Report with Evidence Citations
prompt_templates.prompt: Run a quality assessment on this cohort study and provide the detailed NOS report with a markdown table. For each criterion, cite the specific text from the study that supports your scoring decision.
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs a citable assessment for publication or review

prompt_templates.title: Comprehensive Bias Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Perform NOS quality assessment on this cohort study. Include per-criterion scoring with textual evidence, overall score with quality classification, documented assumptions for uncertain items, and identification of potential bias sources in the study design.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user preparing a detailed methods section for a meta-analysis

output_examples.input: Evaluate the following cohort study: Wang et al. 2018 investigated the relationship between physical activity and cognitive decline in 5,000 adults over 10 years.
output_examples.output: A markdown table evaluating the study against all 9 NOS criteria with stars assigned. The output includes per-criterion reasoning, the total star score, and a quality classification with a brief explanation.

output_examples.input: Assess quality of this cohort study PDF: path/to/study.pdf
output_examples.output: Extracted text from the PDF followed by a structured NOS assessment report. The report includes a markdown summary table, per-criterion scoring with documented reasoning, and an overall quality recommendation.

best_practices: Always provide the full study text or a complete PDF for the most accurate and reliable NOS assessment
Document reasoning for each criterion to make the scoring transparent and auditable by other researchers
When uncertain about a criterion due to missing information, assign a dash (-) rather than guessing
anti_patterns: Do not fabricate study details or NOS scores when study text is missing or incomplete
Do not apply NOS criteria to study types other than cohort studies, such as case-control or cross-sectional designs
Do not skip the reasoning documentation for each criterion as it is essential for scientific credibility

faq.question: What is the Newcastle-Ottawa Scale?
faq.answer: The NOS is a validated tool for assessing the quality of non-randomized studies in meta-analyses. It evaluates cohort and case-control studies across three categories: Selection, Comparability, and Outcome.

faq.question: What scores correspond to different quality levels?
faq.answer: A score of 7-9 stars indicates high quality, 4-6 stars moderate quality, and fewer than 4 stars low quality.

faq.question: Can this skill evaluate case-control studies?
faq.answer: No. This skill only evaluates cohort studies. A separate NOS-based skill designed for case-control studies is needed for that study type.

faq.question: Does this skill access external databases or journals?
faq.answer: No. The skill only works with text or PDF files that you provide. It does not search databases, access paywalled content, or connect to external services.

faq.question: How should I handle studies with missing information?
faq.answer: Assign a dash (-) for criteria where information is missing from the study text. Document the missing information in your report notes for transparency.

faq.question: Can I use this skill with abstracts only?
faq.answer: Abstracts often lack sufficient detail for a full NOS assessment. For best results, provide the complete full-text study.
